home.title: Welcome to SManetting
home.subtitle: Learn to read music, one step at a time.
home.progressEmpty: You haven't started yet. Open the first lesson to begin.

teoria.title: Theory
teoria.subtitle: Progressive lessons: read, listen to the examples and pass the quiz to unlock the next lesson.
teoria.readingDone: When you're ready, test yourself with the quiz.
teoria.question: Question {{n}} of {{total}}
teoria.failed: Not passed: you need at least 80%. Try again!

pratica.title: Practice
pratica.subtitle: Train with interactive exercises. Your progress is saved.

riconosci.title: Name that note
riconosci.desc: Identify the note shown on the staff.

valori.title: Values and rests
valori.desc: Recognize the value of the symbol shown.

armature.title: Key signature quiz
armature.desc: Recognize the major key from the key signature.

dettato.title: Melodic dictation
dettato.desc: Listen to the melody and rebuild it.

ritmo.title: Rhythm training
ritmo.desc: Reproduce the measure in time with the metronome.
ritmo.instructions: Listen to the measure, then tap it with TAP (or spacebar) after the count-in.
ritmo.tapHint: Press TAP or the spacebar in time.

intervalli.title: Interval recognition
intervalli.desc: Listen to (or read) two notes and name the interval.

accordi.title: Chord recognition
accordi.desc: Listen to a triad and identify its type.
accordi.ask: What type of chord is it?

componi.title: Compose
componi.hint: Pick a duration in the palette, then click on the staff: the vertical position sets the pitch. Use the eraser to delete.
componi.hintSelect: Click a note to select it (Shift for multiple), drag it up/down to change its pitch. Arrows ↑↓ to move, Del to delete, Ctrl+Z/Y for undo/redo.

teoria.title: Theory
teoria.subtitle: Progressive lessons with staff examples and short quizzes.

pratica.title: Practice
pratica.subtitle: Interactive exercises with scoring and statistics.

componi.title: Compose
componi.subtitle: A simple but complete score editor.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Your preferences are stored on your device.
settings.englishNotation: Also show English note names (C, D, E…)
settings.englishNotationHint: Note names stay in Italian (Do, Re, Mi…) with the English equivalent next to them.
